title: phegopteris supraspinigera
intro: Phegopteris supraspinigera, also known as the Japanese chain fern, is a species of fern that is native to East Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, such as reducing fever and relieving respiratory issues, due to its expectorant and antiseptic properties. In herbal preparations, Phegopteris supraspinigera is often used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, which can be consumed to leverage its medicinal properties.
